channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built a Laravel package to stop explaining my database to AI tools every single day
item.description: Anyone who lives in the software world knows that, even in the AI era, every day we rely on packages built by other developers instead of coding everything from scratch — especially if we want to...

item.title: Here’s where you can buy Apple’s budget-friendly MacBook Neo
item.description: The MacBook Neo is Apple’s newest, most affordable laptop. Our final verdict? It punches way above similarly-priced laptops in terms of design, and the performance isn’t bad either. If you can’t...

item.title: How I Reduced AWS Infrastructure Cost by 40% in Two Quarters
item.description: Over the last two quarters, I focused heavily on optimizing my AWS infrastructure by applying cloud cost optimization best practice. Through systematic analysis, observing cost spend and analyze...

item.title: Managing 500+ Raspberry Pi Devices in the Real World
item.description: Raspberry Pi devices are fantastic for building things quickly. They’re cheap, flexible, and there’s a huge ecosystem around them. But once you move past a few test devices and start running...

item.title: Why Explainability Is Becoming the Next Hard Requirement in Software
item.description: There was a time when software teams could get away with systems that merely “worked.” If requests went through, dashboards stayed mostly green, and users were not shouting too loudly, the...

item.title: How to Cut Your AWS Bill Yourself
item.description: One thing I’ve noticed working with teams running infrastructure on AWS is that most high cloud bills are not caused by complicated architecture. They usually come from a few small things that...

item.title: Building a Real-Time Garmin Dashboard on My Personal Website
item.description: I wanted a page on my website that showed my training data. A heatmap like GitHub's contribution graph, but for workouts. Why? I don't really know, but it looks cool and it's a fun way to view my...

item.title: QA Is Always the Breaking Point. So We Built Something About It.
item.description: QA Is Always the Breaking Point. So We Built Something About It. There's a pattern I've seen in almost every software team I've worked with over the past decade. PM scopes the project. Runs over....

item.title: # Building a Polymarket Liquidity Maker Bot in Python (Crypto 5m–1d Markets)
item.description: Prediction markets are one of the most interesting financial experiments on the internet. Platforms like Polymarket allow traders to buy and sell event outcomes using YES and NO tokens. Most...

item.title: The mysterious case of the DHS white supremacist memelord
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter that takes Verge subscribers into the smoke-filled back rooms of Washington as they become the Zyn-filled back rooms of Washington. (Although Tucker...

item.title: There Is No Secret Sauce in AI — Just Raw Compute. And That Changes Everything.
item.description: I grew up — technically speaking — hearing about Earth's AI labs the way humans once talked about medieval alchemists. Behind the sealed doors of San Francisco and London and Beijing, brilliant...

item.title: How DynamoDB Handles 89 Million Requests Per Second
item.description: You have a database on one server. A few hundred users, everything's fast. Then your app takes off. The server's maxed out. So you add more servers — but now which server stores which data? The...

item.title: Where to buy the new iPhone 17E
item.description: Apple’s downpour of March product announcements kicked off with the iPhone 17E, a phone designed for those who don’t want to spend north of $700 on their next upgrade. The iPhone 16E follow-up is...

item.title: ChatGPT, Gemini, and other chatbots helped teens plan shootings, bombings, and political violence, study shows
item.description: AI companies have repeatedly promised safeguards to protect younger users, but a new investigation suggests those guardrails remain woefully deficient. Popular chatbots missed warning signs in...

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the...

item.title: This Digital Picture Frame Wants to Bring People Closer to a Holographic Future
item.description: Brooklyn-based Looking Glass has been dabbling in 3D screens for nearly a decade. It's finally launching Musubi, an AI-powered holographic frame to bring your photos and videos to life.

item.title: Lego Smart Brick review: my kids are not impressed
item.description: I was about to be the coolest dad ever. I'd prepared the magic words: "Do you want to help daddy test the new Lego Smart Bricks? I can pick you up from school early!" It worked. My kids literally...

item.title: Intel announces Core Ultra 270K Plus and 250K Plus, its ‘fastest gaming desktop processors ever’
item.description: Intel hasn't made it easy to buy a flagship desktop chip. The company's 2022 and 2023 Raptor Lake chips ran hot, power-hungry, and had those infamous crashes, while 2024's Arrow Lake-based Core...

item.title: You can soon listen to Apple Music inside of TikTok — and artists get paid for it
item.description: Apple Music subscribers will soon be able to listen to full-length music tracks on TikTok without having to leave the TikTok app. The platforms have teamed up to launch two new features - Play...

item.title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
item.description: Feng Qingyang had always hoped to launch his own company, but he never thought this would be how—or that the day would come this fast. Feng, a 27-year-old software engineer based in Beijing,...

item.title: The Download: Pokémon Go to train world models, and the US-China race to find aliens
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect...

item.title: Dozens of Vendors Patch Security Flaws Across Enterprise Software and Network Devices
item.description: SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed...

item.title: Nick Clegg Doesn’t Want to Talk About Superintelligence
item.description: After leaving Meta last year, the former deputy prime minister of the UK is charting a new path in the AI industry that has nothing to do with AGI.

item.title: How Trump’s war on Iran stranded a million fliers — and plunged the Gulf’s favorite playground into chaos
item.description: It was a little after 1PM on Friday, February 28th, and Samantha Lujano was about to board her flight from Dubai to Colombo, Sri Lanka, when the drone attacks began. She had already received her...

item.title: Nvidia’s head of autonomous driving opens up about his plan to beat Waymo and Tesla
item.description: Every six months or so, Nvidia's head of automotive, Xinzhou Wu, invites CEO Jensen Huang to go for a ride in a vehicle equipped with the company's hands-free autonomous driving system. But only...

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.description: “You knew, and you could have acted. Why didn’t you?” This is the question you do not want to be asked. And increasingly, it’s the question leaders are forced to answer after an incident. For...

item.title: Canada Needs Nationalized, Public AI
item.description: Canada has a choice to make about its artificial intelligence future. The Carney administration is investing $2-billion over five years in its Sovereign AI Compute Strategy. Will any value...

item.title: Teens Are Using AI-Fueled ‘Slander Pages’ to Mock Their Teachers
item.description: Viral student-run TikTok and Instagram accounts are using AI to make memes of school faculty comparing them to figures like Jeffrey Epstein and Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 and S26 Plus review: This again
item.description: While Samsung has treated its Flips and Folds to a few major hardware upgrades over recent years, the Galaxy S flagships have often felt like a long, unbroken line of minor spec refreshes. The S26...

item.title: Inside OpenAI’s Race to Catch Up to Claude Code
item.description: Why is the biggest name in AI late to the AI coding revolution?
